formula_note: Low = recovered bodies; best = official figure reported in the mission-report archive note; high = Tokyo fire-department estimate.
uncertainty_note: All three figures aim at the same-night direct-death event, but they capture the dead with different completeness rules.
quality_note: Strong same-night civilian direct-death band for a tightly bounded episode.

formula_note: Low and best use the city-health / USSBS injury figure of 40,918; high uses the fire-department wound estimate.
uncertainty_note: Auxiliary only; not mixed into baseline death estimates.
quality_note: Useful as context but not needed for baseline DR.

uncertainty_note: Population is approximate ('around 1.1 million') and the one-day window makes the rate very sensitive.
quality_note: Bounded denominator, but still approximate and suitable only for fallback intensity.

formula_note: Fallback civilian intensity metric = civ_deaths_direct / civ_person_months × 1,000,000.
uncertainty_note: This produces very large values because the episode lasts roughly one day. It is retained only as a transparent fallback intensity, not a DR.
quality_note: Fallback-only intensity metric for a one-night event.
